validated_commands.benchmark: uv run py.test tests/test_processing.py tests/test_filters.py --codspeed -k benchmark --override-ini='addopts='

open_prs.title: [Perf Improver] perf: avoid np.mean overhead in _calc_momentary and _calc_short_term

optimization_backlog.desc: RunningSum.add() - bypass property setters for direct attribute access
optimization_backlog.notes: Profile shows 3900 calls taking 5ms over 50 iterations. Saving ~30% = 30us/iteration. Not worth a standalone PR.

optimization_backlog.desc: For very long sessions (>10000 blocks), bisect.insort list shift may become bottleneck
optimization_backlog.notes: Could use a sorted container library or a different algorithm (e.g., histogrammed approximation)

optimization_backlog.desc: _process_quarter_block: use np.einsum for sq_sum instead of np.sum(np.square(...))
optimization_backlog.notes: Saves ~1.7us per call on quarter block. 2300 blocks = ~4ms over 50 iters. Small but measurable.

perf_notes: np.mean has ~4us fixed overhead regardless of array size due to numpy dispatch machinery
np.quantile is extremely expensive for small arrays due to Python dispatch overhead
pytest-codspeed (simulation mode) benchmarks with --override-ini=addopts= to disable xdist
cProfile shows _calc_lra was 39% of process_block time before incremental LRA optimization
np.mean on 3-element array: 4200ns; direct arithmetic: 360ns (11.7x faster)
test dataset for benchmark: 1770-2_Comp_RelGateTest_prefiltered_gated.npz at 48000 Hz, ~46 blocks
checked_off_items: perf-assist/incremental-lra PR (checked off in monthly activity 2026-04)
